zh.lifeSummary: 當Matilda de Ferrers於1395年出生在英國英格蘭萊希斯特斯爾格洛比時，她的父親William Ferrers 5th Barron Ferrers of Groby23歲，母親Philippa Clifford24歲。她和John Raleigh至少有2個兒子和1個女兒。她去世於1462年在英國英格蘭享年67歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Ferrers
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: locative name from Ferrières-Saint-Hilaire (Eure) and Ferrières (Manche).

variants.name: Ferris
meanings: Irish and Scottish: shortened Anglicized form of Irish Ó Fearghuis or Ó Fearghasa ‘descendant of Fearghus’, or from the Scottish-Gaelic form of this personal name, Fearghus (see Fergus ).
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Ferrières-Saint-Hilaire, Eure and Ferrières, La Manche in Normandy, France.

variants.name: Ferridge
meanings: English: unexplained; possibly a form of Ferris and therefore a variant of Ferrers .

nameMeanings.name: Matilda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

variants.name: Mathilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

en.lifeSummary: When Matilda de Ferrers was born in 1395, in Groby, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom, her father, William Ferrers 5th Barron Ferrers of Groby, was 23 and her mother, Philippa Clifford, was 24. She had at least 2 sons and 1 daughter with John Raleigh. She died in 1462, in England, United Kingdom, at the age of 67.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Matilda de Ferrers est née en 1395, à Groby, Leicestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, William Ferrers 5th Barron Ferrers of Groby, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Philippa Clifford, avait 24 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille avec John Raleigh. Elle est décédée en 1462, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 67 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Matilda de Ferrers 1395 in Groby, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William Ferrers 5th Barron Ferrers of Groby 23 und ihre Mutter Philippa Clifford 24. Sie hatte mit John Raleigh mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1462 im Alter von 67 Jahren in England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Matilda de Ferrers è nata il nel 1395 nel Groby, Leicestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, William Ferrers 5th Barron Ferrers of Groby, aveva 23 anni e sua madre, Philippa Clifford, aveva 24 anni. Lei avuto almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia con John Raleigh. Morì nel 1462, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 67 anni.
